title: Superconductivity and nematic order in a new titanium-based kagome metal CsTi3Bi5 without charge density wave order
type1_summary: The inspected files are all figure/source-data style XLSX tables with physical-variable headers or direct numeric plotting structure, consistent with cleaned, replot-ready datasets.
type2_summary: No clear raw/unprocessed instrument-output files were present among the inspected files.

classification.reasoning: Although the underlying measurement is raw XRD, the file itself is a cleaned, replot-ready tabular export of the figure data. Per the dataset definitions, source-data style figure deposits are Type 1.
classification.paper_evidence: Fig. 1b is an XRD pattern of an as-grown single crystal, explicitly described as a raw measurement in the paper, but the deposited file is organized as figure data for plotting.
classification.file_evidence: XLSX table with clear headers '2θ' and 'Intensity(arb. units)', 2 columns x 8501 rows, small figure-sized dataset directly plottable as an XRD curve.
classification.ambiguity: Paper calls the measurement raw, but the file structure is replot-ready source data rather than an instrument output file.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports a new titanium-based kagome metal, CsTi3Bi5, synthesized as high-quality single crystals and found to superconduct below an onset Tc of about 4.8 K without exhibiting charge density wave order. Using transport, magnetization, STM/STS, Josephson STM, QPI imaging, and DFT, the authors show two superconducting gaps and a rotational-symmetry-breaking nematic electronic state that persists in both superconducting and normal states.

figures.description: Schematic crystal structure of CsTi3Bi5 showing Ti kagome layer, Bi layers, and Cs layers.
XRD pattern of an as-grown CsTi3Bi5 single crystal with (00l) reflections and inset photo of a hexagonal crystal.
Laue diffraction pattern along [001], demonstrating crystallinity.
Atomic-resolution HAADF-STEM image along [210] with structural model overlay.
Zero-field-cooled magnetic susceptibility versus temperature under various magnetic fields for H//c, showing superconducting diamagnetic transition.
Ultra-low-field magnetic susceptibility showing strong field sensitivity and increased superconducting volume fraction.
Resistivity versus temperature below 5 K showing superconducting transition and zero resistance.
STS dI/dV spectrum showing a large superconducting gap Δ1.
Josephson STS spectrum of the large gap using a superconducting tip, showing zero-bias Josephson peak.
STM topography of a Bi-terminated surface with honeycomb lattice and spatial region used for gap mapping.
Linecut of dI/dV spectra showing spatially uniform large gap Δ1 across the surface region.
STS dI/dV spectrum showing a small superconducting gap Δ2.
Josephson STS spectrum of the small gap using a superconducting tip.
Linecut of dI/dV spectra showing spatially uniform small gap Δ2 across the surface region.
STM topography of a Bi-terminated surface associated with the small-gap region.
Schematic of angular-dependent STS with in-plane magnetic field rotated in the kagome plane.
dI/dV spectra at different in-plane field angles showing angle-dependent superconducting gap Δ1.
Angular dependence of Δ1 showing twofold symmetry.
Polar plot of Δ1 versus angle, showing the nematic axis alignment.
dI/dV spectra at different in-plane field angles showing angle-dependent superconducting gap Δ2.
Angular dependence of Δ2 showing twofold symmetry.
Polar plot of Δ2 versus angle, showing the nematic axis alignment.
Panorama of dI/dV spectra for the small gap at different field orientations, showing symmetry breaking beyond the gap scale.
Large-area STM topography T(r) of the Bi surface with point defects.
Fourier transform T(q) of the topography showing six Bragg peaks.
dI/dV map g(40 mV, r) around point defects showing QPI.
Fourier transform g(40 mV, q) of the dI/dV map showing C2-symmetric QPI patterns and angular intensity analysis.
Energy-dependent FT QPI maps g(E,q) showing disappearance of C2 features away from the Fermi level.
Calculated constant-energy contours from DFT at 40 meV with orbital labels and scattering vectors.
Simulated QPI based on the calculated constant-energy contours with isotropic spectral weight.
Simulated QPI with orbital-dependent anisotropic spectral weight reproducing C2 symmetry.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include XRD patterns, Laue diffraction images, HAADF-STEM images, EELS/EDS spectrum images, SEM-EDS composition data, magnetic susceptibility curves, resistivity curves, STM topography images, STS dI/dV spectra, Josephson STM spectra, dI/dV maps for QPI, and angular-dependent magnetoresistance measurements. The paper states these were collected with specific instruments and conditions, but does not provide raw file formats in the text.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: Processed plots include linecuts of dI/dV spectra, extracted superconducting gap values versus angle, polar plots of gap anisotropy, Fourier transforms of STM/STS maps, angular intensity profiles of QPI, and DFT-based constant-energy contours and simulated QPI. The paper also mentions drift correction of STM maps using the Lawler-Fujita algorithm and fitting of tunneling spectra with the Dynes function.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states that the raw data supporting the plots of the main figures are provided in the Source Data file, and additional data measured or analyzed during the study are available from the corresponding author upon request.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described as the measurements underlying the main figures, but the text does not specify file formats, directory structure, or whether all raw instrument outputs are included. The paper mentions measurements from XRD, SQUID magnetometry, PPMS transport, STM/STS, Josephson STM, STEM, EELS, EDS, and DFT calculations.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include extracted superconducting gap values, averaged gap sizes from many spectra, linecuts, Fourier-transformed QPI images, angular dependence curves, AMR ratios, and DFT-derived constant-energy contours and simulated QPI. The text indicates some spectra were normalized and some STM maps were drift-corrected before Fourier analysis.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Yes. The paper explicitly says source data are provided for the main figures, which implies figure-specific data tables/files rather than only narrative descriptions. Supplementary figure data are mentioned but not explicitly stated as included in the source data file.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts or notebooks are mentioned in the paper text.
dataset_characterization.data_organization: figure-based source data plus unspecified additional data on request; raw folder structure not specified
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: Main-figure source data are explicitly provided, so the plotted values are likely available for replotting. However, the paper does not specify whether full raw instrument files or analysis scripts are included, so exact reproduction of processing steps may not be possible from the text alone.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper distinguishes between raw measurements and derived quantities: e.g., STM topographs and dI/dV maps are raw measurements, while Fourier transforms, extracted gap-versus-angle curves, and simulated QPI are processed/derived. The source data statement refers only to main figures, not to supplementary figures or analysis code. The Methods section also notes use of drift correction and Dynes fitting, indicating some preprocessing and model fitting before plotting.
